messages.content: Go ahead and commit it - I trust you, don't let me down.

As agreed, I committed the L3 quality-gate metrics and the Prometheus bridge, plus the 1.3 evaluation harness (eval subpackage + akg-eval CLI + samples) as two focused commits: aa8f1ef and 390f149. Not pushed; working tree clean.
Then continue - strictly follow plan/rules/code_rules.md.

Advanced Phase 2: swapped the AKG shared pool from memorystore.New() to postgres/sqlite (DI swap). Added AKGStore and AKGSQLitePath to config; added newSharedAKGStore / newPostgresAKGStore / newSQLiteAKGStore to bootstrap; postgres open failure degrades gracefully to memory. Full test suite green. Not committed per code_rules section 10.4.

Phase 3 (A2: wire leader agent auto-retrieval) must first pass the 1.3 real-sample hard gate (structure at least 95 percent, precision at least 85 percent), and the exact injection point should be re-confirmed via grep when Phase 3 starts (candidate: the leader agent's retrieval/context-build step, not SearchSimilarTasks at production_manager_tasks.go line 255). Run the tests first, then review.

Ran akg-eval on built-in samples: ready_for_phase3 true, structure equals 1.0, precision equals 1.0. Wrote review doc AKG_PHASE3_REVIEW.md. Located injection point at internal/agents/leader/agent_memory.go line 63 BuildContext, called by agent.go line 383 and agent.go line 706. retriever.New is only called in tests repo-wide - no production agent path yet.
Compress the context - I'll run it myself.

Here is the runbook: for synthetic samples use go run ./cmd/akg-eval -dir internal/ares_memory/compiler/eval/samples; for real samples, drop files (messages plus candidates plus optional gold) in a directory following conv_clean.json format, then run. gold is optional - the precision gate only runs when gold is present. Two things pending your confirmation: the real-sample hard-gate measurement, and the write/read coupling decision for StoreProvider registration versus kc.Enabled.
I ran the clean samples: structure_pass_rate equals 1.0, precision_micro equals 1.0, ready_for_phase3 true, samples equals 4.

Found that LoadDir is non-recursive, so it silently skips noisy/conv_noisy.json - all 4 samples are top-level clean data; gates all pass but the noise dimension has zero coverage. Suggest running -dir noisy alone to verify L1 filtering, or making LoadDir recursive.

I ran the noise sample: nodes_in equals 6, dropped_low_conf equals 1, dropped_structural equals 1, objects_built equals 4, structural_pass_rate equals 0.833, precision_micro equals 1.0, structure_gate false, ready_for_phase3 false, exit code 3.

The noise sample failing the gate is expected: the structure_rate metric counts L1-correctly-discarded malformed nodes as structural failures too. But L1 filtering does work (dropped_low_conf equals 1, dropped_structural equals 1). Entity n4 'the' with confidence 0.6 entered the store with an empty key - a known L1 gap that must be fixed before wiring Phase 3.
Then do option A - I forgot, what were we even doing?

Timeline: Phase 2 done, not committed; Phase 3 readiness review; found the LoadDir non-recursive pitfall; conclusion that 1.3 legitimately passes on clean samples. Executed option A to fix the L1 empty-key/stopword issue: added 'the' to akgStopwords in validator.go, added an entityResidual guard to the NodeEntity branch, promoted the reasonStopwordEntity constant, and updated tests accordingly. Full test suite green, not committed.
Also, the conversation sample - just export this conversation's content as the sample, go.

The harness scores candidates, not messages (Message is for provenance only). To make the conversation a valid sample we must supply extracted candidates plus gold. Confirmed NewAKGExtractor().Extract consumes a slice of SourceMessage directly, with a self-contained normalizer and no nil risk.
I mean: structurally export the context of this very conversation, and we will use it as the test sample.
Agreed on fixing the extractor's Chinese extraction quality (or tightening L1 to catch degenerate run-ons), then re-test 1.3. Remember to update the dialogue into the doc each session as test corpus.

Root cause of run-ons located: all come from the English-capitalized word path in extractEntities, where strings.Fields splits English prose adjacent to other text without whitespace into a single token (e.g. 'SearchSimilarTasks). then you test it first'), and any capitalized word is treated as an entity; the Chinese dictionary/suffix path is clean. Fixed: the English path now rejects hasCJK tokens and non-ASCII identifiers (including code-call brackets like NewAKGExtractor().Extract); added the isASCIIIdentifier helper. compiler package build/vet/lint/test all green. Per your request, appended this turn to the conversations/ourchat.json corpus, re-ran gen_real.go with the real AKGExtractor to regenerate samples_real/ourchat.json, extended gold, and re-tested 1.3. Not committed (code_rules section 10.4).
I had a requirement: not to fit to the real data, but to fix the mechanism. And the output - you should remember it is in English, right? I mean the exported dialogue. Let's fix the dialogue first.

facts: Phase 2 swaps memorystore with postgres or sqlite
Phase 3 requires a real sample hard gate
leader agent builds context via BuildContext
extractor run-on is fixed by isASCIIIdentifier
